methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Code Metal, a Series B defense and security company founded by Peter Morales and Alex Showalter-Bucher, has raised $161M to date against a current post-money valuation of $1.25B. The peer universe consists of 10 comparable companies: 2 public firms (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities in the same sector and stage, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods anchor the valuation analysis. Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $1.2B last post-money valuation, implying $2.4B. Peer-median analysis draws from seven same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $300M to $1.5B; the cohort median is $1.0B. The peer set demonstrates material dispersion, with top-quartile peers (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI) valued at $1.4B–$1.5B against the stage median of $1.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $850M (low) to $2.7B (high), with a median of $1.7B. Code Metal's current $1.25B valuation sits below the median, closer to peer benchmarks but above the bottom quartile. Key uncertainties include the breadth of revenue traction relative to peers and whether the company commands step-up multiples consistent with stage-progression expectations or should anchor closer to peer medians. The 3.2x range between low and high reflects material stage-specific and peer-dispersion risk.
